title: A-priori C^{3/4,0} bound (l:apriori) and lambda_n-dependence bookkeeping (c:hmain, L:main)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where:** Lemma `l:apriori`, Corollary `c:hmain`, and assembly of `L:main`.

`l:apriori` bootstraps $[\phi]_{\mathcal X}\le C\|b\|_{\mathcal C^{-1}}(1+\|\rho\|+\ell^{1/2}[\phi]_{\mathcal X})$ and iterates $N\sim\ell^{-1}$ times. It relies on a-priori finiteness $[\phi]_{\mathcal X}<\infty$ (justified only by '$b$ bounded').

**Gap to verify.** In `c:hmain` applied to $\tilde v$, the feedback enters as $\rho=\lambda_n(u-\tilde v)$, giving $[\tilde v-V]_{\mathcal C^{3/4,0}}\le C(1+\|h\|^2)(1+\lambda_n\|u-\tilde v\|_{\mathcal C^{0,0}L_m})$ — a **linear** $\lambda_n$ growth. The whole quantitative argument hinges on this $\lambda_n$ factor cancelling against the $\lambda_n^{-1/8}$ gains in `c:finfin` to produce the $\lambda_n^{-1/8}(\lambda_n^{-1}+\|u-\tilde v\|)$ shape of `sdeb2`. This bookkeeping (the quantitative heart of `L:main`) should be checked line-by-line, including the a-priori finiteness of $[\phi]_{\mathcal X}$ for $\tilde v$.
